page: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance from the N1 family. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor using x86_64 architecture with hyper-threading enabled. The instance is equipped with 624 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 6.5 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, and has zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks reveal a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core performance is weak, as shown by bottom-quartile scores in stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests, alongside weak single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. However, the massive core count delivers strong multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is a key strength, achieving top-quartile results in both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached read/write operations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high resource density for memory-bound applications, though it relies on an older Intel Xeon architecture that lacks modern instruction sets like AMX or AVX-512 VNNI. This makes it a cost-effective choice for legacy multi-threaded workloads and in-memory caching rather than modern AI training. It is highly suited for large-scale database operations, Redis deployments, and multi-threaded data processing, while being less optimal for single-threaded tasks or real-time LLM text generation.
description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-96 is an x86_64 memory-optimized instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 624 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon processor with 48 physical cores. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs. Benchmarks indicate weak single-core performance but strong multi-core capabilities and exceptional memory bandwidth, with top-quartile results in stress-ng multi-core tests and uncached read/write operations. This resource-dense configuration is highly efficient for memory-bound, highly parallelized workloads. It is best suited for large databases, Redis caching, and multi-threaded data processing, though it is less effective for single-threaded applications or real-time LLM text generation.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-96 is an x86_64 instance with 96 vCPUs and 624 GB of RAM. It delivers strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth for database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n1-highmem-96 features 96 vCPUs, 624 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, making it ideal for large databases and Redis.
tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized virtual machine engineered for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and highly parallelized multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 624 GB of RAM powered by Intel Xeon processors
Strong multi-core performance and exceptional memory bandwidth
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Weak single-core and single-threaded execution speeds
Optimized for large databases, Redis caching, and parallel processing
